chapters.title: What is Java?
chapters.content:
Java is a high-level, object-oriented programming language designed by James Gosling at Sun Microsystems (now Oracle) in 1995.

Java's famous motto: Write Once, Run Anywhere (WORA). Java code compiles to bytecode which runs on the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) — so the same code runs on Windows, Linux, macOS, or any device with a JVM.

Java is used for:
  • Android app development
  • Enterprise backend systems
  • Web servers and APIs
  • Desktop applications
  • Big data tools (Hadoop, Spark)

chapters.title: Hello World
Data Types
OOP — Classes & Objects
Inheritance & Interfaces
Collections
Exception Handling
Useful Tips
chapters.content:
String methods:
  s.length()          length
  s.toUpperCase()     ALL CAPS
  s.toLowerCase()     all lower
  s.trim()            remove whitespace
  s.contains("x")     check substring
  s.replace("a","b")  replace chars
  s.split(",")        split to array
  s.charAt(0)         character at index

Math class:
  Math.max(a, b)
  Math.min(a, b)
  Math.abs(x)
  Math.sqrt(x)
  Math.pow(x, y)
  Math.random()    // 0.0 to 1.0

Casting:
  int x = (int) 3.99;      // x = 3
  double d = (double) 5;   // d = 5.0
  String s = String.valueOf(42);
  int n = Integer.parseInt("42");

Print shortcuts:
  System.out.println(x);    // print + newline
  System.out.print(x);      // no newline
  System.out.printf("Hi %s, you are %d", name, age);
